title: 046b EGYENSULYBAN / Babushka Masha - H
description:
The true cost of life never shows up in a balance sheet. Babuschka Mascha: "I had no choice" "Babuschka Mascha" is about simple and hard facts of life being exposed as much as Babuschka Mascha dares to open up. This documentary is not about politics - no poor female soul glorified, no painting rosy a horrible past or turning a horrific life into a happy end. It is a drama of emotional life, a process starting somewhere and ending someplace, somehow,...when we decide to stop asking questions. There is a kind of secret in all this I did not mention yet: Integrity. Though life looks like a washing machine in reverse - the laundry getting more dirty the longer the washing is on - where people end upside down in the process, their soul in hell, their values corrupted. But when the door of the "Lifewasher" finally opens and confusion as well as destruction look unbearable, there seems to be some sort of integrity surviving in all this that takes my breath. 

camera Jürg Da Vaz, Tatyana Khalzova, Irlan Saginov 
coordination in Almaty Dinazat Kassimova 
organization in Kostanay Tatyana Khalzova 
director of photography Jürg Da Vaz 
edited by Sândor Gréczi, Jürg Da Vaz 
directed by Jürg Da Vaz 
filmed on location in Kostanay, Kazakhstan in May 2003 
a handmade Jürg Da Vaz production 2003 

Email from Tatyana Khalzova 24.03.2006, 10:09:58 GMT+01:00 City: Kostanay Country: Kazakhstan 
"Hi Jürg, How do you do? I stayed at grandmother Masha recently. There were relatives, we all look at 'BABUSHKA MASHA' film. They looked and smiled. They liked the film. I think the film is a document about history. Babushka Masha says hello and invites you to visit her again. 
Tatyana
